source: Local Streamlit app (app/streamlit_app.py) served on localhost, captured through controlled Chromium browser sessions.
privacy: All assets use only synthetic inputs (public reference profiles / artifacts.example_input()). Synthetic demonstration probabilities are intentionally visible; no real BRFSS row, user upload, probability associated with a real/user row, target, or split index appears in any asset.

assets.name: 01-landing-desktop.png
assets.purpose: Prediction-first landing: title, orientation caption, the three-section navigation (Individual selected), and the individual 21-field input form.
assets.synthetic_source: none (empty default form; no data entered)
assets.alt_text: Streamlit app landing page showing the Diabetes Risk Estimator title, a three-option navigation radio (Individual prediction selected, Batch CSV prediction, About & architecture), and the two-column yes/no health indicator form.

assets.name: 02-individual-result-desktop.png
assets.purpose: Individual estimate with the SHAP local explanation and the scenario explorer at rest, for a synthetic higher-risk case.
assets.synthetic_source: synthetic higher-risk profile entered by the capture script (HighBP, HighChol, Smoker, coronary heart disease, difficulty walking; BMI 42, age 75); no real dataset or user row.
assets.alt_text: Individual prediction result showing an estimated probability of 38.8%, a SHAP contribution bar chart with an Increased/Decreased legend, a list of the largest contributions, and the model scenario explorer, above the medical disclaimer.

assets.name: 03-scenario-comparison-desktop.png
assets.purpose: One-field model-behavior scenario comparison showing a non-zero difference for the same synthetic case.
assets.synthetic_source: same synthetic higher-risk profile; scenario input 'Physical activity' changed from No to Yes.
assets.alt_text: Model scenario explorer comparing the original estimate (38.8%) with a hypothetical scenario (39.7%), a difference of +0.90 percentage points, with a non-causal notice that the comparison describes model behavior only.

assets.name: 04-batch-workflow-desktop.png
assets.purpose: In-memory batch workflow with a synthetic mixed-validity CSV: 8 rows, 6 valid and 2 invalid, plus the privacy notice and probabilities-only warning.
assets.synthetic_source: CSV of eight artifacts.example_input() rows with two BMI cells deliberately set to 'invalid'; no real dataset or user row.
assets.alt_text: Batch CSV prediction workflow showing a summary of 8 total rows, 6 valid and 2 invalid, a result preview table with two rows marked invalid, download buttons, and a warning that the output contains probabilities only.

assets.name: 05-about-mobile.png
assets.purpose: About & architecture section on a narrow viewport: what the estimator does and does not do, how input becomes a probability, model-behavior and population-level framing, and links to versioned evidence.
assets.viewport: mobile (390 x 812 CSS px; five overlapping viewport segments stitched after capture)
assets.alt_text: About & architecture section on a mobile-width layout, listing what the estimator does and does not do, how an input becomes a probability, how to read the explanation and scenario, the population-level fairness audit note, and where the technical evidence lives.
